title: conium maculatum var. divaricatum
intro: Conium maculatum var. divaricatum, also known as giant hemlock, is a variant of the conium maculatum plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant contains a number of bioactive compounds, including coniine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Conium maculatum var. divaricatum has been used to treat a range of health issues, including muscle and joint pain, as well as respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves, and is said to provide relief from symptoms such as bronchitis, asthma, and arthritis.
